claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 12 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)

Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children would you like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my two children. I have Alex, who is 12, and Blake, who is 6.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to know you have two children, Alex (12) and Blake (6).

Now, could you please tell me: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

Next question: **Do both Alex and Blake live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, actually Blake lives with me, but Alex doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to ask a couple of follow-up questions about their situation.

**Regarding Alex (who doesn't live with you): Do you contribute towards Alex's upkeep (for example, by paying for food, clothes, pocket money, or other expenses)? If so, is the amount you contribute at least equal to the Child Benefit rate (currently £25.60 per week for an eldest child)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I do contribute towards Alex's upkeep - I help out with things like clothes and school bits - but it's not as much as that Child Benefit rate you mentioned. I give about £20 a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that detail. Since your contribution is less than the Child Benefit rate, I have one more question about Alex:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, such as the person Alex lives with?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not sure if the person Alex lives with is claiming it or not. I haven't really discussed that with them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. That's helpful context.

Just a couple more questions to complete the picture:

**Regarding Blake (who lives with you): Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, actually someone else is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake. They live with Blake as well.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. So Blake lives with both you and another person who is already claiming Child Benefit for them. That's helpful to know.

Just to confirm my understanding: **Are you and this other person (who is claiming Child Benefit for Blake) living in the same household together, such as a partner or spouse?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - we live in the same household together.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. That makes sense.

I have just a couple more questions to ensure I have the full picture:

1. **Are either of your children (Alex or Blake) in any of the following situations:**
   - In local council care
   - In hospital for an extended period
   - Receiving benefits in their own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)
   - Working 24 hours or more per week

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex has actually been in hospital for about 3 months now. But I still contribute what I can - buying things like magazines, their favorite snacks, things like that. Blake isn't in any of those situations though.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. I'm sorry to hear Alex has been in hospital for such a long time.

Since Alex has been in hospital for more than 12 weeks (3 months), I need to ask a follow-up question:

**Before Alex went into hospital, were you the one claiming Child Benefit for them, or was it someone else (such as the person Alex normally lives with)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I wasn't claiming Child Benefit for Alex before they went into hospital.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake. Let me summarise what I've gathered and provide my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I've completed my assessment of your eligibility for Child Benefit.

---

## Summary of Eligibility

### Alex (age 12) — **Not Eligible**
Unfortunately, you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. There are a couple of reasons:

1. **Alex doesn't live with you:** To qualify for Child Benefit for a child who lives elsewhere, you would need to contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit rate (currently £25.60 per week) towards their upkeep. You mentioned you contribute around £20 per week, which is below this threshold.

2. **Extended hospital stay:** Alex has been in hospital for approximately 3 months (over 12 weeks). While exceptions can allow Child Benefit to continue after 12 weeks if you "regularly spend money on them," this exception applies to the person who was *already* claiming Child Benefit before the hospital stay — which wasn't you.

---

### Blake (age 6) — **Not Eligible**
You would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake because someone else in your household is already claiming Child Benefit for them. Only one person can receive Child Benefit per child. 

However, if you and the other person agree, they could stop their claim and you could make a new claim yourself. This would need to be arranged between you both.

---

I hope this information is helpful. If your circumstances change — for example, if you increase your contributions for Alex to at least £25.60 per week, or if you and your partner decide you should be the one claiming for Blake — you may wish to revisit your eligibility. If you have any questions about the process, I'd recommend contacting the Child Benefit Office for further guidance.

reasonings: Alex (age 12): You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex for several reasons. Firstly, Alex does not live with you. To qualify for Child Benefit for a child who lives with someone else, you would need to contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit rate (currently £25.60 per week) towards their upkeep - you confirmed you contribute approximately £20 per week, which is below this threshold. Additionally, Alex has been in hospital for approximately 3 months (more than 12 weeks). While there are exceptions that allow Child Benefit to continue after 12 weeks in hospital if you 'regularly spend money on them', this exception applies to the person who was already claiming Child Benefit before the hospital stay - which was not you. Since you were not claiming Child Benefit for Alex before hospitalisation and your contributions fall below the required threshold, you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake (age 6): You are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake because someone else in your household is already claiming Child Benefit for them. Only one person can receive Child Benefit for a child. Since Blake lives with both you and the other person who is already claiming, and that person is already receiving Child Benefit, you cannot make a separate claim. If you wanted to become the person who receives Child Benefit for Blake, you would need to agree with the other person in your household for them to stop their claim, and then you could make a new claim yourself.
arguments.overall_reasoning: Based on the information provided, you are not currently eligible to claim Child Benefit for either of your children. For Alex, you do not meet the contribution threshold required when a child lives elsewhere, and the extended hospital stay rules do not apply in your favour since you were not the previous claimant. For Blake, someone else in your household is already claiming Child Benefit, and only one person can receive this benefit per child.

For Alex, you do not meet the contribution threshold required when a child lives elsewhere, and the extended hospital stay rules do not apply in your favour since you were not the previous claimant. For Blake, someone else in your household is already claiming Child Benefit, and only one person can receive this benefit per child.
